name: Marvin Minsky
key_contributions: Co-authored "Perceptrons" (1969) with Seymour Papert — demonstrated limitations of single-layer neural networks
Developed the Society of Mind theory of intelligence
Built the SNARC — one of the first neural network machines (1951)
Pioneered frames as a knowledge representation scheme
summary: Marvin Minsky was one of the most influential and controversial figures in AI history. He co-founded the MIT AI Lab and made fundamental contributions to knowledge representation, robotics, and the theory of mind. However, his 1969 book Perceptrons, co-authored with Papert, demonstrated the limitations of single-layer perceptrons and is widely credited with triggering the first AI winter by discouraging neural network research for over a decade.
